free_text: Neutron yields from a thick C-12 and Be-9 target
irradiated by 50 and 65-MeV deuterons.
University Louis Pasteur.
Reaction lines are corrected according to
remarks of N. Otsuka
.Correction in subentries 004 and 005
was made, according N.Otsuka remarks.
The hardness parameters of the neutron spectra
are calculated for the radiation damage studies of the
silicon devices.
For higher neutron energies from 4 to 70-MeV
three position sensitive neutron detectors were
used.  The detectors were placed at distance 171-cm
from the target at different angles relative to the
beam axis.  Each detector consist of a diameter
5.5-cm*100-cm quartz tube filled with NE-213
scintillation liquid.
The low-energy parts of the spectra from 0.3 to 10 MeV
were measured in a separate run by using three modules
of low-threshold neutron detectors positioned at the
same angles but closer to the target.
K-130 accelerator
*By authors. The correction for accidental coincidences
in measured neutron tof spectra the transformation to
neutron energies was performed using the relativistic
equation.
The data were received from authors.
The C-12 target-a 25-mm thick cylinder, 48-mm in
diameter was directly irradiated without any additional
shielding. The target was mounted on a 1.5-mm thin
aluminum window at the end of a 10-cm long tube that
was isolated from the rest of the beam line and served
as a Faraday cup.
A cylindrical 14-mm, 50-mm diameter Be-9 target was
covered by a 3.1 mg/cm**2 Al foil to prevent chemical
contamination from beryllium evaporation and
sputtering. The target was mounted on a 1.5-mm thin
aluminum window at the end of a 10-cm long tube that
was isolated from the rest of the beam line and served
as a Faraday cup.
The uncertainty is reported by authors in
table 2.
